Examples

  • Gallery

stackY

stackY is a layout operator that cumulates weighted segments within a group and normalizes them to fill an extent. Each mark occupies [cumsum − weight, cumsum] of its group's total: y (segment TOP) = cumsum/total, h (segment height) = weight/total. Bind both with .pick("y","h"). The cumulative and the total are WINDOWED sums (the same window family as rank), so it lowers to SQL. Because the outputs are RELATIVE [0,1] shares, the frame STRETCHES each group's segments to fill its box — a percent-stack per group. Replace barY with stackY on the y axis of a bandX chart to get stacked bars.

const stacks = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(stacks, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", group: "species", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
}).axis("x");
return v;

Stacked bars: bandX places one bar per species; stackY partitions each bar's total by island and cumulates the segments to fill the height.

Options

stackY is apply-only (no trained fit) — its outputs are within-group relative shares in [0,1], so there is no natural axis over it. Its constructor takes one bag of INPUT columns (all are marks columns):

input required meaning
weight yes the segment's magnitude (summed into the cumulative and total)
group no the PARTITION — the x category the bars stack within
order no the stacking sequence within a group; default = weight

With no group, all rows form one stack (which is also the absolute stack). With no order, segments stack by weight (largest last).

Outputs (bind with .pick(...)):

output space meaning
y relative coord (y) segment TOP (cumsum / total)
h relative len (y) segment height (weight / total)

Examples

Stacked bars — segments partitioned by island, ordered by island name:

const stacks = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(stacks, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", group: "species", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
}).axis("x");
return v;

Percent stack — every bar fills the full height because shares normalize to each group's total, so stackY IS a normalized (100%) stack by construction:

// Same spec as above: each species bar reaches y=1 (100%); the segment split shows composition.
const stacks = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(stacks, {
  ...bandX("species").pick("x", "w"),
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", group: "species", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
}).axis("x");
return v;

Custom stacking order — sequence segments by an explicit column instead of by weight:

const stacks = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.rect(stacks, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.2 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", group: "species", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
}).axis("x");
return v;

Single stack (no group) — one column of cumulated segments over all rows:

const byIsland = db.table("penguins").groupby(["island"], { n: count() });
const v = canvas(320, 460);
v.rect(byIsland, {
  x: 40, w: 120,                              // one fixed column (pixels)
  ...stackY({ weight: "n", order: "island" }).pick("y", "h"),
  fill: ordinal("island"),
});
return v;

See also

  • stackX — the x-mirror; grouped stacked segments → {x,w} (horizontal stacked bars).
  • pitchY — the contrast: fixed-PITCH pixel bands, extent-INDEPENDENT (this stackY fills an extent; pitchY does not).
  • bandX — the x placement each stacked bar sits in.
  • barY — the un-stacked single-value alternative for a bar's height.
